name: Apple Cider Vinegar (Raw)
description: A fermented vinegar from apple juice containing acetic acid, malic acid, and beneficial bacterial cultures ('the mother'), used primarily as a hair rinse and scalp treatment.
chemicalName: Aqueous solution of acetic acid with malic acid and microbial cultures
appearance: Amber to golden-brown liquid; raw versions contain visible cloudy strands ('mother')
solubility: Fully miscible with water in all proportions
chemistry: Acetic + malic acid. 'Mother' contains beneficial bacteria. Restores scalp pH, closes cuticle, dissolves buildup.
